note: Daily MarCRM scrape — 4 pr-marketing leads driven by May 19 funding announcements (Eisen, Transient.AI, Runware, Armada) + 3 legal-freelance contract postings (Major Lindsey & Africa fund formation, two Latitude Legal contract roles). All deduped against reports from May 14-19. Signal refresh on Bunch confirms the May 19 Series B trigger — pitch immediately.

leads.name: Eisen
signals: Series A $10M led by MissionOG announced May 19, 2026 — total raised now $18.5M
Existing customers include Adyen, Binance.US, BitGo, OKX, PeoplesBank — credible logo set for media
Index Ventures, Cowboy Ventures, First Round Capital, Restive Ventures backed seed; MissionOG led A
Compliance ops infrastructure for fintechs holding customer funds — clear B2B story
Monitors ~$16B across tens of millions of accounts at ~50 companies — strong proof data
Estimated $700M in crypto escheated in 2026 — TAM narrative for thought leadership
leads.notes: Classic post-raise PR moment: regtech narrative, fintech buyers, sophisticated investor list, big-name customers. Pitch a 90-day comms sprint or retainer on the $700M crypto escheatment narrative + the Adyen/BitGo customer logos. CEO Allen Osgood is a credible spokesperson. PE/VC-backed fintech with B2B buyers fits the pr-marketing ICP cleanly.

leads.name: Transient.AI
leads.industry: AI for institutional capital markets (regulated trading workflows)
leads.fundingStage: Series A (May 18-19, 2026) — led by NEXT Investors
signals: Series A announced May 18-19, 2026, led by NEXT Investors (capital-markets-infra PE firm)
Use of funds explicitly: 'accelerate global go-to-market expansion' across Americas, EMEA, APAC
AI Operating System for institutional trading — front-to-back office workflow
PE backing implies installed-base playbook and credibility-sensitive sale — fits agency ICP
NYC-headquartered, B2B financial services — exact fit for Mark's PR & marketing pipeline
leads.notes: Strong post-raise lead. Global GTM expansion explicitly named in use-of-funds → they need press across three regions on a compressed timeline. PE-backed institutional trading platform = high-credibility sale, retainer-friendly. Pitch a launch comms + media-relations engagement for the Americas → EMEA → APAC sequence.

leads.name: Runware
leads.fundingStage: Series A — $50M led by Dawn Capital; $66M total to date
signals: $50M Series A led by Dawn Capital; participation from Comcast Ventures, Speedinvest, Insight Partners, a16z speedrun
10B+ generations powered for 200k+ developers — strong proof data for press
Plans 20+ inference PODs across EU + US cities in 2026 — infrastructure build-out narrative
Goal: 'one API for all AI' — strong category story
Sonic Inference Engine claims 30-40% perf gains vs rivals, 5-10x cost cut — defensible metrics
leads.notes: Slightly weaker on intent than Eisen/Transient because announcement was December 2025 with continuing press May 2026. But the inference-POD build-out gives a recurring narrative arc — could pitch ongoing retainer to tell the story of each new POD opening. Developer-tools audience overlaps with B2B SaaS messaging.

leads.name: Armada
leads.industry: AI infrastructure / modular data centers (defense, energy, mining)
signals: Series B $230M at $2B pre-money valuation announced May 19, 2026
Co-led by Overmatch, 8090 Industries, BlackRock (new investor)
New strategic investors: Johnson Controls, NightDragon, Mitsui, Singtel Innov8
400,000-sq-ft Arizona factory partnership with Johnson Controls — 500+ jobs
Customer bookings grew 540% FY25→FY26; Q1 FY27 up 2,000% YoY
Sells into US military, mining, telecom, oil & gas — multi-vertical narrative
leads.notes: Borderline fit — defense/military exposure may not be Mark's preferred client profile, and BlackRock-backed unicorns typically have established comms. Including as B-priority because the Johnson Controls factory partnership, the 540% / 2,000% growth metrics, and the multi-vertical AI-infra narrative are all strong PR hooks. Worth a low-effort outreach to assess fit before committing capacity.

leads.name: Major, Lindsey & Africa — Interim Fund Formation Attorney (Remote, 4+ months PT)
leads.industry: Legal staffing — interim placement for a law firm client
leads.compensationText: Not stated publicly; MLA Interim Legal Talent typically markets in $150-275/hr range
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Interim / part-time contract via Major, Lindsey & Africa
signals: Posting explicitly says 'immediate need' for an Interim Fund Formation Attorney
10+ years fund formation required — squarely in Mark's wheelhouse
Engagement is 4+ months part-time — recurring revenue, not one-shot
Client is a law firm — overflow / specialist support pattern Mark targets
Securities Act of 1933, '34 Exchange Act, '40 Act / Advisers Act fluency required
leads.source: Major, Lindsey & Africa via LinkedIn / ZipRecruiter
leads.notes: A-grade lead. MLA Interim is exactly the channel partner pattern in the Mark ICP — they place fractional/interim counsel into law firms and operating companies. Apply ASAP; previous MLA interim postings (5/18) closed quickly. Resume framing: '10+ years private-funds + 1940 Act fluency, available for 4+ month remote engagement.'

leads.name: Latitude Legal — Corporate Transactional Attorney (Silicon Valley software company, Remote)
leads.industry: Legal staffing — Silicon Valley enterprise software client
signals: Posting flagged 'immediate need' on Latitude careers page
Annualized comp $250k-$300k+ — top-of-market for contract corporate work
Silicon Valley software company client — high-velocity corporate transactions expected
Latitude tends to roll postings every 60-90 days; this one is fresh in May 2026 batch
leads.notes: Strong intent score — Latitude is a proven freelance channel that has placed corporate transactional attorneys for years. Comp is at the top of the band, and 'immediate need' urgency is rare. Fit drops because it's corporate, not fund-formation, but the M&A/financing exposure that came with Latitude #5077 last week suggests this client is highly active.

leads.name: Latitude Legal — M&A Attorney, Global Diversified Conglomerate (Remote Contract)
leads.industry: Legal staffing — global diversified conglomerate in-house team
leads.employmentTypeRaw: Long-term contract, fully remote, potential conversion to permanent
signals: Long-term contract with potential conversion to permanent — recurring relationship, not project
Global conglomerate's in-house team — implies steady transactional flow
Annualized $175-190k = market-credible band for contract corporate counsel
Latitude has a strong placement track record — high probability of close
leads.notes: Solid B-grade lead. Long-term + remote + market comp + Latitude's vetting bar. Less urgent than the Silicon Valley software client posting above. Worth applying as a hedge if the Latitude #5077 (Securities/Corporate Governance) from 5/19 doesn't move.
